title: Reactive inhibition of pore structure collapse during pyrolytic formation of carbon molecular sieves
abstract: Disclosed herein are carbon molecular sieves and methods of making the same through the pyrolysis of a polymer precursor in the presence of a reactive gas stream including a hydrogen source.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A process for controlling a pore structure of a carbon molecular sieve comprising: providing a polymer precursor; heating the polymer precursor in a chamber to at least a temperature at which the polymer precursor undergoes pyrolysis; and flowing a reactive gas stream through the chamber during the heating. 2. The process of claim 1, wherein the reactive gas stream includes a source of hydrogen. 3. The process of claim 1, wherein the reactive gas stream includes Â¾. 4. The process of claim 1, wherein the reactive gas stream further comprises an inert gas selected from a group consisting of argon, neon, N2, helium, and CO2 or combinations thereof. 5. The process of claim 1, wherein the reactive gas stream comprises Â¾ and argon. 6. The process of claim 1, wherein the temperature of the pyrolysis of the polymer precursor film is from 500 Â°C to 1500 Â°C. 7. The process of claim 1, wherein soak time of the pyrolysis of the polymer precursor film is from 30 min to 24 hours. 8. The process of claim 1, wherein the reactive gas stream is a pure Â¾ stream. 9. The process of claim 1, wherein the Â¾ is in a concentration of from 1 ppm to 4 vol.% of the reactive gas stream. 10. The process of claim 1, wherein the polymer precursor comprises a rigid polymer. 11. The process of claim 10, wherein the polymer precursor comprises a rigid, microporous polymer. 12. The process of claim 11, wherein the rigid microporous polymer is a polymer of intrinsic microporosity selected from a group consisting of PIM-1, PIM-7, P1M-8, RÎªM-9, KAUST-PI-1, PIM-BADAS-1, PIM-DUCKY-1, PIM-Tz 25 , PIM-DUCKY-2, PIM-BADAS-2, PIM-SADAS, and combinations thereof. 13. The process of claim 1, wherein the carbon molecular sieve is utilized as a membrane, adsorbent, catalyst, composite or a filter. 14. The process of claim 1, wherein the polymer precursor has a form factor of film, sheet fiber, hollow fiber, coated tube, coated disc, or coated monolith. 15. The process of claim 1, wherein an inert gas stream flows through the chamber during the heating, wherein the inert gas stream comprises argon, and wherein flow rate of the inert gas stream is different from flow rate of the reactive gas stream. 16. The process of claim 1, wherein the polymer precursor comprises PIM-1. 17. The process of claim 1, wherein ramp rate of the process is from 0.1 Â°C/min to 200 Â°C/min. 18. The process of claim 1, wherein cool down rate of the process is from 0.1 Â°C/min to 200 Â°C/min. 19. The process of claim 1, wherein the reactive gas stream reacts with the polymer precursor to form ThO and/or CO2 during pyrolysis. 20. The process of claim 1, wherein the chamber comprises a fume hood comprising a tubular furnace, a quartz tube disposed at least partially inside of the tubular furnace, a mesh 

 plate support disposed inside of the quartz tube, and the polymer precursor is disposed on the mesh plate support. 21. The process of claim 1, wherein ultra-micropores of the carbon molecular sieve are selectively targeted by the pyrolysis to prevent collapse while leaving the micropores relatively unchanged. 22. The process of claim 1, wherein the diffusion selectivity of the polymer precursor is enhanced while the sorption selectivity of the polymer precursor is essentially unchanged. 23. The process of claim 1, wherein an Â¾ concentration and/or pyrolysis temperature is selected to obtain a desired molecule permeance or perm-selectivity. 24. A carbon molecular sieve membrane produced according to the process of claim 1. 25. The carbon molecular sieve membrane of claim 24, including ultra-micropores from 5 A to 20 A. 26. The carbon molecular sieve membrane of claim 24, wherein a sp 3 /sp 2 hybridized carbon ratio in the membrane is from 0.1 to 1.0. 27. The carbon molecular sieve membrane of claim 24, wherein the surface area is from 2 m 2 /g to 1000 m 2 /g. 28. The carbon molecular sieve membrane of claim 24, having a / xylene permeability fourteen times larger than a -xylene permeability of a membrane prepared without Â¾ during the heating. 29. The carbon molecular sieve membrane of claim 24, having a /-xylene permeability of rÂ· n r rrwlxm molxm from 8.5 x 10 1 â m 2 - -xsxPa to 8.5 x 10 13 â m 2 -xsxPa . 30. A module comprising a plurality of carbon molecular sieves produced according to the process of claim 1 and used as an adsorbent bed or a membrane bed. 31. The module of claim 30, wherein the adsorbent bed and the membrane bed are used for gas and vapor separations, aqueous separations, organic separations, and hydrocarbon separations. 32. A process for performing a separation on a feed stream, comprising the module of claim 30, wherein the feed stream comprises a first component and a second component, to form a permeate stream enriched in the first component and a retentate stream depleted in the first component. 33. The process of claim 32, wherein the feed stream comprises the first component in concentration range of 1 wt.% to 99 wt.%. 34. The process of claim 32, wherein the feed stream is a natural gas stream, the permeate stream is enriched in the first component including at least one of CO2, Â¾S, H2O, and He, and the retentate stream is enriched in the second component including at least one of CÂ¾ and N2. 35. The process of claim 32, wherein the feed stream comprises a Cx aromatics stream, the permeate stream is enriched in the first component including at least one of benzene and p- xylene, and the retentate stream is enriched in the second component including at least one of ethyl benzene, o-xylene and m-xylene. 36. The process of claim 32, wherein the feed stream comprises a virgin naphtha stream (IBP-380 Â°F), the permeate stream is enriched in the first component including at least one of n-paraffms and aromatics, and the retentate stream is enriched in the second component including at least one of iso-paraffins and cyclo-paraffins. 37. The process of claim 32, wherein the feed stream comprises a whole crude stream, the permeate stream is enriched in the first component including at least one of naphtha (IBP-380 

 Â°F) and kerosene fractions (380-530 Â°F), and the retentate stream is enriched in the second component including at least one of the remaining fractions (530 Â°F+). 38. The process of claim 32, wherein the feed stream includes a heavy hydrocarbon stream having bitumen, atmospheric resid, vacuum resid, steam cracker tar, and/or fluid catalytic cracker main column bottom, the permeate stream is enriched in the first component including at least one of saturates and/or 3- ring aromatics, and the retentate stream is enriched in the second component including at least one of 3+ ring aromatics, asphaltenes, metals, and/or micro-carbon residue.
